subject: Ecology
title: The Sargasso Sea
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Sargasso Sea usable.

hook.text: In the middle of the Atlantic, a sea exists with no coastline—only floating seaweed.

buildup.text: The Sargasso Sea is bounded by currents, not land. Sargassum weed drifts on its surface.

discovery.text: The weed creates a floating habitat for fish, crabs, shrimp, and baby sea turtles.

twist.text: European and American eels migrate thousands of miles here to spawn—then die.

climax.text: The Sargasso shows that ecosystems don't need solid ground to thrive.

punchline.text: A sea defined by seaweed, not shores.

quiz.question: What makes the Sargasso Sea unique?
options: It has no coastline, only floating seaweed
It is the deepest sea in the world
It has no marine life at all
